items.title: Model selection and hyperparameter tuning
items.description: Validation error reflects bias and variance together; choosing simpler models or stronger regularization when validation lags training targets high-variance overfitting.

items.title: Ensemble methods (bagging, random forests)
items.description: Averaging predictions from models trained on bootstrap samples reduces Var(ŷ) while preserving much of the base learner's expressiveness.

items.title: Data collection strategy
items.description: Learning curves show whether more training data would mainly shrink variance; investing in data often beats endlessly increasing model capacity.

items.title: Production monitoring after retraining
items.description: High-variance models may perform well on one training snapshot but drift when retrained on the next batch—monitor score variance across retrain cycles.
